title: [InstCombine] Fix behavior for `(fmul (sitfp x), 0)`
summary: The developers generalized a scalar optimization into a robust pattern by identifying the core constraint—non-zero constant checking for signed integer conversions—and extending it systematically. They first expanded from scalar to vector types, ensuring the transform handles splat and partial undef vectors correctly. They then validated edge cases like negative zero and non-zero constants, confirming the logic works for all FP constant forms. Finally, they applied the same principle to related operations (like fadd with signed constants) to catch similar missed optimizations. The key insight was isolating the essential condition (non-zero check for signed conversions) and applying it consistently across variations, while using undef-aware matching to maintain safety. This approach turns a single-case fix into a reusable transformation that prevents similar oversights in vectorized and edge-case scenarios.
